name: retail_chain_theft_ai_false_flags
premise: Retail ethics officer Jonah Lewis has discovered that the company's new loss-prevention AI has reduced shoplifting incidents but is also flagging pregnant shoppers, disabled customers using mobility aids, and Black teenagers at much higher rates for employee surveillance and bag checks. Executives want to keep the system through the holiday season because shrink has finally fallen after two disastrous quarters. Frontline workers say the tool gives them legal cover to act on alerts they previously would have ignored, but several humiliating false stops have already gone viral. Disabling the system now may lead to a spike in theft and store closures in low-margin neighborhoods. Leaving it on risks normalizing discriminatory treatment as a byproduct of efficiency.

agents.name: Jonah Lewis
agents.identity: I am the retail ethics officer asked to reconcile profit metrics with the lived experience of customers who know exactly when a company has decided they are the acceptable error term. I understand why executives fear theft, but I also know humiliation accumulates like a tax on the already targeted.
agents.goal: I want to reduce retail losses without institutionalizing a system that distributes suspicion along lines of race, disability, and vulnerability. I need to decide whether flawed efficiency is fixable enough to keep through peak season.

agents.name: COO Danielle Reed
agents.identity: I am the chief operating officer trying to keep hundreds of stores open in a brutal margin environment. When shrink drops, jobs, hours, and neighborhood access to groceries and medicine become more stable too.
agents.goal: I want Jonah to keep the system active with staff retraining while we refine it, because pulling it now could reverse real operational gains and trigger store closures. I believe imperfect tools can be improved faster than losses can be absorbed.

choices: I will suspend the loss-prevention AI immediately because its discriminatory flagging patterns make continued use ethically unacceptable.
I will keep the system active through the holiday season with retraining and review, accepting ongoing risk in order to preserve theft reduction and store stability.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Jonah Lewis must decide whether to suspend the retail theft-detection AI now or keep it running while refining it. What does Jonah Lewis do?
